title: Gomortega keule
taxonomy: Gomortega keule is classified as a species within the family Gomortegaceae. This family consists of a small number of species and is endemic to the temperate forests of Chile. Gomortega keule is a deciduous tree that is native to the Valdivian rainforests of Chile. Its classification at the level of kingdom and phylum is Plantae, belonging to the clade Angiosperms. The specific classification of Gomortega keule is Gomortega keule Kunth.

intro: Gomortega keule, also known as the Copiapoa or Copayapoa, is a small, deciduous tree native to Chile, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The health benefits of Gomortega keule include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
